target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
AIR is a Series A transportation company founded by Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, and Netanel Goldberg, having raised $23M to date. The broader Transportation sector includes eight private peers—among them REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger—with no public comparable companies available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation analysis is constrained by limited peer density at AIR's stage and tier. The Peer-Median method was inapplicable: while five same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer median unreliable. The Stage-Progression method was therefore the sole applicable triangulation approach. Using AIR's $23M total raised as a proxy for implied post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-round progression yields an implied valuation of $46M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a midpoint of $46M. This range reflects conservative application of a single method and carries meaningful uncertainty stemming from the absence of robust peer comparables. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier absent granular data on Transportation-specific Series A expansion multiples, and the limited visibility into AIR's unit economics, growth trajectory, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set. Additional diligence on operational metrics and peer valuations would be necessary to narrow this range.
